selected_important_turn.text: Hun,It's a bit of a fair deal,but due to the condition here i will need a bit more of water though foo cannot be overlooked also for survival but still to survive we take chances.So do you mind me giving you a package of food and u trading 2 pakages of water?.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn is the key turning point: the opponent had just made a cooperative trade suggestion (willing to trade food for water), and this reply moves the interaction from cooperative information-sharing into a competitive, claim-driven stance. That shift sets the tone for the allocation and narrows the space for integrative trade.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The offer is rigid and self-favoring (asking for 2 water for 1 food) despite both parties signaling high need for water. It ignores other goods (firewood) and fails to explore multi-issue tradeoffs or clarify available quantities. Because it is competitive and imprecise, it risks escalating, reducing joint value, or prompting a hard counter-offer.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Stay cooperative while advocating for your water need: acknowledge their offer, ask clarifying questions (how many packages available / how many do you need?), and propose multi-item packages that create tradeoffs (e.g., include firewood or split water more evenly). Offer a more balanced exchange or ask what trade would make them comfortable rather than demanding 2-for-1 outright.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — exploring multi-issue packages and quantities can create integrative trades rather than locking into zero-sum splitting
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — a more balanced, clarified proposal would appear fairer to both parties
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — asking questions and offering flexible packages lowers resistance and speeds agreement
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase — maintaining a cooperative tone builds trust and avoids the adversarial shift

value2reason.Low: I have enough food to get by. More would be nice, but it's a luxury.
value2reason.Medium: It gets cool at night and I want a big fire, but I don't want to spend time collecting it.
value2reason.High: I like hiking and need more water to stay hydrated while active.

value2reason.Low: We need additional package of this item to sustain us and give us nourishment and maintain our well being.
value2reason.Medium: Actually this is needed to make fire for food and most importantly to warm ourselves during cold weathers
value2reason.High: The main reason we need additional packages of this item is because it sustains even wen all other constraints are unavailable

dialogue.text: Can i kindly know your preferences please?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Well, I'm kind of low on water, so I'd like to get as much of that as I can. I'd like to go hiking and I don't really have enough water for a long trip. What about you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Ohh nice.I also have the same issue around here.The thirst for water is growing high than ever due to drought
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Well, that's a bummer. How are you set for food? While I wouldn't mind a bit more, I'm in pretty good shape there, so I'd be willing to trade it for water. I'd hate to leave without getting a good hike in.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Hun,It's a bit of a fair deal,but due to the condition here i will need a bit more of water though foo cannot be overlooked also for survival but still to survive we take chances.So do you mind me giving you a package of food and u trading 2 pakages of water?.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I appreciate the offer, but I need to prioritize water. How many packages do you need for food, and what trade would make you comfortable? Let’s find a balanced exchange.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm okay with trading 1 water for 1 food. It gives you more water while I get some food without sacrificing too much. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I see your point. If you’re willing to trade 1 water for 1 food, maybe we can also include some firewood to balance the deal. How about 1 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood for 2 waters? That way, we both get what we need without losing too much.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
